What is a DAO? Imagine a company where every shareholder votes on every decision. No CEO, no board of directors. Just code and consensus. That's a DAO.
Liquidity pools explained: you deposit two tokens into a pool. Traders swap against your deposit. You earn a share of the fees. It's like being a mini exchange.
NFTs aren't just jpegs. They're proof of ownership on the blockchain. Think of them as digital property deeds that nobody can forge.
A blockchain is just a database that nobody owns but everyone can verify. That single idea is what makes it revolutionary.
Smart contracts are programs that run on the blockchain. Once deployed, nobody can change them. The code does exactly what it says, every time.
Layer 2 solutions are like express lanes on a highway. Same destination, less traffic, lower tolls. They process transactions off the main chain and batch them back.
Never share your seed phrase. Not with customer support. Not with friends. Not with anyone. Write it down on paper and store it somewhere safe offline.
Hot wallet vs cold wallet: a hot wallet is connected to the internet, making it convenient but more vulnerable. A cold wallet is offline, safer but less convenient for daily use.
What is tokenomics? It's the economics of a token. Supply, demand, distribution, and utility. Good tokenomics means the math works long-term.
Staking is like putting your crypto in a savings account. You lock it up to help secure the network, and in return you earn rewards.
Myth: Bitcoin is anonymous. Reality: Bitcoin is pseudonymous. Every transaction is public and traceable. Your identity isn't attached, but your address history is visible to everyone.
Web2: you are the product. Your data gets sold to advertisers. Web3: you own the product. Your data belongs to you. At least, that's the vision we're building toward.
Gas fees are the cost of using a blockchain network. Think of it like postage for sending a letter, except the price changes based on how busy the post office is.
What is a blockchain fork? When developers disagree on how to update the code, the chain can split into two. Bitcoin Cash was born from a Bitcoin fork.
DeFi stands for Decentralized Finance. It's the idea that you can lend, borrow, trade, and earn interest without a bank or broker in the middle.
A private key is like the master password to your crypto wallet. Anyone who has it can access your funds. Protect it like you'd protect the keys to a safe.
What is yield farming? You move your crypto between different DeFi protocols to earn the highest possible returns. High reward but also high risk.
Proof of Work is like a math competition. Miners race to solve complex puzzles. The winner gets to add the next block and earn rewards. It requires lots of energy.
Proof of Stake is different. Instead of solving puzzles, validators lock up their coins as collateral. They're chosen to validate blocks based on how much they stake.
An oracle in crypto brings real-world data onto the blockchain. Smart contracts can't access the internet on their own, so oracles feed them prices, weather data, and more.
What is a DEX? A decentralized exchange lets you trade crypto directly from your wallet. No account needed, no middleman. Just connect your wallet and swap.
Impermanent loss happens when the price of tokens in your liquidity pool changes compared to when you deposited. It's the hidden cost of providing liquidity.
A bridge in crypto lets you move tokens between different blockchains. Moving ETH from Ethereum to Polygon, for example. Useful but bridges have been targets for hacks.
What is a hash? It's a mathematical function that converts any data into a fixed-length string of characters. Change one letter of the input and the output changes completely.
The mempool is where unconfirmed transactions wait before being added to a block. Think of it as a waiting room for your crypto transfer.
What is a sidechain? It's a separate blockchain that runs alongside a main chain. It has its own rules but can communicate with the main chain for security.
Token burning means permanently removing tokens from circulation. It's like buying back shares and destroying them. It can increase scarcity and potentially value.
What is an airdrop? A project distributes free tokens to existing users or wallet holders. It's a marketing strategy and a way to bootstrap a community.
Consensus mechanisms are how a blockchain network agrees on which transactions are valid. Bitcoin uses Proof of Work. Ethereum switched to Proof of Stake.
What are rollups? They bundle hundreds of transactions together, process them off-chain, and then post the results back to the main chain. Faster and cheaper.
A multisig wallet requires multiple signatures to authorize a transaction. Like a safety deposit box that needs two keys from different people to open.
What is TVL? Total Value Locked measures how much crypto is deposited in a DeFi protocol. Higher TVL generally indicates more trust and usage.
Flash loans let you borrow millions of dollars in crypto without collateral, as long as you repay it within the same transaction. They exist only in DeFi.
What is a node? A computer that maintains a copy of the blockchain and helps validate transactions. More nodes means more decentralization and security.
The difference between a coin and a token: a coin has its own blockchain, like Bitcoin or Ethereum. A token is built on someone else's blockchain, like USDC on Ethereum.
What is ERC-20? It's a technical standard for creating tokens on Ethereum. It ensures all tokens follow the same rules so wallets and exchanges can support them easily.
Decentralization isn't binary. It's a spectrum. Some projects are more decentralized than others. The key is understanding where on that spectrum your project sits.
What is a block? A collection of transactions that gets added to the blockchain. Each block contains a reference to the previous block, creating an unbreakable chain.
MEV stands for Maximal Extractable Value. It's the profit that miners or validators can make by reordering transactions in a block. It's a complex and controversial topic.
What is slashing? In Proof of Stake systems, if a validator misbehaves or goes offline, a portion of their staked tokens gets destroyed as punishment.
Atomic swaps let you exchange one cryptocurrency for another directly, without an exchange or intermediary. The swap either happens completely or not at all.
What is a governance token? It gives holders the right to vote on changes to a protocol. Think of it as shares in a company, but for decentralized projects.
The blockchain trilemma says you can only optimize for two of three things: decentralization, security, and scalability. Every blockchain makes trade-offs.
What is a wrapped token? It's a token that represents another crypto from a different blockchain. Wrapped Bitcoin (WBTC) lets you use BTC on Ethereum.
Zero-knowledge proofs let you prove something is true without revealing the underlying information. You can prove you're over 18 without showing your birthdate.
What is composability? In DeFi, protocols can be combined like building blocks. One protocol's output becomes another's input. It's why DeFi is called money legos.
On-chain vs off-chain: on-chain means data is recorded directly on the blockchain. Off-chain means it's stored elsewhere. Each has trade-offs in cost and security.
What is a liquidity provider? Someone who deposits tokens into a pool so others can trade against them. In return, they earn a portion of the trading fees.
Account abstraction is changing how crypto wallets work. Instead of managing private keys yourself, your wallet can have custom rules like spending limits and recovery options.
What is finality? It's the guarantee that a transaction can't be reversed once confirmed. Different blockchains achieve finality at different speeds.
The difference between Layer 1 and Layer 2: Layer 1 is the base blockchain like Ethereum. Layer 2 is built on top to handle more transactions without congesting the base layer.
What is a whitepaper? A technical document that explains how a crypto project works. It outlines the problem, the solution, the technology, and the tokenomics.
Merkle trees are a data structure used in blockchain. They let you verify that a specific transaction is included in a block without downloading the entire block.
What is a validator? In Proof of Stake, validators are responsible for checking transactions and adding new blocks. They stake their own tokens as a guarantee of honesty.
Cross-chain interoperability is the ability for different blockchains to communicate with each other. It's one of the biggest challenges in the crypto space right now.
What is a cold storage? It's keeping your crypto completely offline, usually on a hardware device or even on paper. Maximum security for long-term holdings.
The difference between permissioned and permissionless blockchains: permissionless means anyone can participate, like Bitcoin. Permissioned means you need approval to join.
What is a DEX aggregator? It searches across multiple decentralized exchanges to find you the best price for your trade. Like a flight comparison site but for crypto.
Tokenization means converting real-world assets into digital tokens on a blockchain. Real estate, art, stocks, anything can potentially be tokenized.
What is a nonce? A number used once in cryptography. In Bitcoin mining, miners change the nonce to find a hash that meets the difficulty requirement.
State channels let two parties transact off-chain as many times as they want, and only settle the final result on the blockchain. Faster and cheaper.
What is a seed phrase? A sequence of 12 or 24 words that can recover your entire wallet. It's the master key to all your crypto. Lose it and you lose everything.
The difference between fungible and non-fungible: one dollar is the same as any other dollar, that's fungible. A specific piece of art is unique, that's non-fungible.
What is a block reward? The crypto given to miners or validators for successfully adding a new block to the blockchain. Bitcoin's block reward halves every four years.
Sharding divides a blockchain into smaller pieces called shards. Each shard processes its own transactions in parallel. It's how Ethereum plans to scale massively.
What is a soft fork vs a hard fork? A soft fork is backward-compatible. A hard fork is not, meaning old nodes can't validate new blocks. Hard forks can split a chain.
DAOs handle billions of dollars using just smart contracts and community votes. No CEO needed. The code enforces the decisions the community makes.
What is a testnet? A blockchain network used for testing. Developers try out their code with fake tokens before deploying to the real network. It prevents expensive mistakes.
Blockchain explorers let you view every transaction on a public blockchain. You can see wallet balances, transaction history, and smart contract activity. Complete transparency.
What is ENS? The Ethereum Name Service turns your long wallet address into a readable name like yourname.eth. Much easier to share and remember.
Bonding curves are mathematical formulas that determine token prices based on supply. As more tokens are bought, the price goes up according to the curve.
What is a rug pull? When a project's developers drain the liquidity pool and disappear with everyone's money. Always research the team before investing.
Soulbound tokens are non-transferable NFTs that represent credentials, achievements, or identity. They can't be bought or sold, only earned.
What is a blockchain timestamp? Every block records the time it was created. This creates a permanent and tamper-proof record of when each transaction occurred.
Optimistic rollups assume transactions are valid by default and only run computations if someone challenges them. It's a clever approach to scaling.
What is staking delegation? If you don't want to run a validator node yourself, you can delegate your tokens to an existing validator and share in their rewards.
Protocol revenue is the fees a DeFi protocol earns from users. It's like a company's revenue but generated entirely by smart contracts with no employees.
What is a liquidity crisis? When there aren't enough tokens in a pool for traders to buy or sell at reasonable prices. Prices can spike or crash rapidly.
Data availability is one of the hardest problems in blockchain. Nodes need to verify that all the data for a block actually exists without downloading all of it.
What is a sandwich attack? A bot sees your pending transaction, places a buy order before yours and a sell order after. You get a worse price. MEV at its worst.
Blockchain immutability means once data is written, it can never be changed or deleted. That's both its greatest strength and its most challenging limitation.
What is a relay chain? In networks like Polkadot, the relay chain is the central chain that provides security and enables communication between different parachains.
Self-custody means you hold your own private keys and are solely responsible for your crypto. Not your keys, not your coins. It's the fundamental principle of crypto.
What is time-weighted average price? TWAP is a strategy that breaks large trades into smaller ones over time to minimize price impact. Common in DeFi protocols.
Decentralized identity lets you prove who you are without relying on a central authority. Your identity data lives on the blockchain, controlled by you.
What is a genesis block? The very first block of a blockchain. Block zero. For Bitcoin, it was mined by Satoshi Nakamoto on January 3, 2009.
Programmable money sounds futuristic but it already exists. Smart contracts can automatically split payments, enforce conditions, and execute agreements without anyone in the middle.
What are dynamic NFTs? Unlike static NFTs, they can change based on external conditions. A sports NFT could update with real-time stats. The possibilities are huge.
Blockchain pruning is the process of removing old transaction data that nodes no longer need. It keeps the blockchain manageable in size while preserving integrity.
What is a payment channel? A pathway between two users to send transactions back and forth off-chain. The Lightning Network is built on this concept.
Verifiable randomness is crucial for fair blockchain applications. From lotteries to NFT drops, you need randomness that nobody can manipulate or predict.
What is a recursive proof? It proves the validity of multiple proofs at once, like a proof of proofs. It makes zero-knowledge systems much more efficient.
Social recovery wallets let trusted friends help you regain access if you lose your key. No seed phrase needed. It makes crypto more accessible to normal people.
What are inscriptions? Data permanently stored on a blockchain, like images on Bitcoin. Ordinals brought NFT-like functionality to Bitcoin without changing its core protocol.
The modular blockchain thesis says you don't need one chain to do everything. Separate chains for execution, settlement, and data availability working together.
What is account abstraction? It turns your wallet into a smart contract, enabling features like gas sponsorship, batch transactions, and custom authentication.
Intents are a new transaction paradigm. Instead of specifying exact steps, you declare what you want. The system figures out the best way to execute it.
What is a validity proof? It mathematically proves that a batch of transactions was processed correctly. ZK-rollups use validity proofs instead of fraud proofs.
Decentralized storage means your files are spread across many computers instead of one server. No single point of failure. IPFS and Filecoin work this way.
What is a precompile? Built-in functions on a blockchain that perform common operations more efficiently than regular smart contract code. They save gas.
Token standards like ERC-721 for NFTs ensure interoperability. Any wallet or marketplace that supports the standard can work with any token following it.
What is a blob? In Ethereum's upgrade roadmap, blobs are temporary data attached to blocks specifically for Layer 2 rollups. They make rollup transactions much cheaper.
Cryptographic signatures prove you authorized a transaction without revealing your private key. It's like signing a document in ink, but mathematically verifiable.
What is a liquidity bootstrap pool? A way for new tokens to establish a fair starting price. The ratio between two tokens shifts over time during the launch period.
Blockchain analytics tools can trace the flow of funds across wallets and exchanges. Privacy on public blockchains is more limited than most people realize.
What is sequencing? In rollups, a sequencer orders transactions before they're processed. Who controls the sequencer matters a lot for decentralization.
Blockchain timestamps create an immutable record of when something happened. This is useful far beyond crypto, from supply chains to legal documents.
What is a commit chain? A blockchain that regularly posts proof of its state to a parent chain. It gets security from the parent while handling its own transactions.
Public goods in crypto are projects that benefit everyone but are hard to fund. Gitcoin uses quadratic funding to support open-source development.
What is a dead man's switch in crypto? A smart contract that automatically transfers your crypto to designated recipients if you stop interacting with it for a set period.
Decentralized science, or DeSci, uses blockchain to fund research, share data, and publish papers without traditional gatekeepers. Science for everyone.
What is a fee market? When more people want to use a blockchain than it can handle, fees increase. Users bid to have their transactions processed first.
Blockchain can solve the double-spending problem. Before Bitcoin, there was no way to prove a digital asset hadn't been copied and spent twice.
What are verifiable credentials? Digital certificates stored on-chain that can be verified by anyone without contacting the issuer. Think diplomas, licenses, certifications.
Threshold signatures split a private key into multiple shares. A transaction requires a minimum number of shares to sign, adding security without complexity.
Web3 gaming lets players truly own their in-game items as NFTs. You can trade, sell, or take your items to a different game. Your loot is actually yours.
What is a state channel network? Multiple connected state channels that can route payments through intermediaries. It enables fast payments between any two users.
Real world asset tokenization is bridging traditional finance and DeFi. Treasury bills, real estate, commodities, all represented as tokens on a blockchain.
Blockchain can provide transparency in charitable giving. Donors can track exactly how their money is used, from donation to final expenditure.
